items.html: After ZON received from the root of Hochma, too, they do not need to suckle any longer. Then ZON descend in their vessel, the root Hochma rises to her place, and the root of Keter gathers a part of the light to it. That Keter at the end of the roots receives only the vitality it needs. Now he is called Matei [expanding] in the Keter to the vessel since the light returned to its vessel. However, the root of the upper Keter is called not Matei below in Akudim. Thus, we have concluded from all this that the light in the first vessel is called Keter, having only light of Hochma since the light of Keter remained above, at the end of the roots. This is the meaning of, “In wisdom You have made them all.”
